title: The Principal And The Pauper

jokes.text: Good Lord! The rod up that man's butt must have a rod up its butt.
jokes.setup: Willie commenting on Principal Skinner's rigid demeanor
jokes.punchline: Escalating the metaphor to absurd proportions - a rod within a rod
jokes.explanation: Perfect character voice for Willie, brilliant escalation of a common metaphor, maximum economy with layered absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Groundskeeper Willie
dialogue.line: Aye, the man's got a rod up his back so far, there's another rod up that rod's back!

jokes.text: Superintendent Chalmers, can I offer you a cup of coffee-flavored Bevarine?
jokes.setup: Formal offering of refreshment to superior
jokes.punchline: Made-up corporate product name 'Bevarine' suggests cheap substitute
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Skinner's institutional mindset and budget constraints

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: Can I offer you some Bevarine? It's a new drink that combines the refreshing taste of beer and margarine.

jokes.text: Yes. I take it gray, with Creamium.
jokes.setup: Normal coffee order response
jokes.punchline: 'Creamium' - fake chemical-sounding name for creamer, plus 'gray' coffee
jokes.explanation: Matches Skinner's corporate speak with equally artificial response, efficient wordplay

dialogue.speaker: Superintendent Chalmers
dialogue.line: I'll have coffee with 'creamium' and sugar.

dialogue.speaker: Barista/Server
dialogue.line: You mean creamer?

dialogue.speaker: Superintendent Chalmers
dialogue.line: No, 'creamium' - it sounds more scientific.

dialogue.speaker: Superintendent Chalmers
dialogue.line: And make the coffee extra gray.

jokes.text: The teachers' lounge is for teachers, Willy.
jokes.setup: Willie tries to participate in announcement
jokes.punchline: Obvious statement delivered with bureaucratic authority
jokes.explanation: Simple authority joke, gets job done but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Superintendent Chalmers
dialogue.line: The custodian's microphone privileges have been revoked. Effective immediately.

jokes.text: Miss Hoover, which one is 'oral'?
jokes.setup: Teacher asks for volunteers for oral report
jokes.punchline: Ralph's innocent confusion about basic vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph moment - innocent confusion creating uncomfortable double meaning

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: Who would like to volunteer for an oral report?

dialogue.speaker: Ralph Wiggum
dialogue.line: Ooh! Ooh! I'll do the oral report!

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: Excellent, Ralph. Your topic is 'What does oral mean?'

dialogue.speaker: Ralph Wiggum
dialogue.line: Oral? Like, with your mouth?

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: Yes, Ralph. Oral means spoken.

dialogue.speaker: Ralph Wiggum
dialogue.line: I'm not supposed to talk about my mouth.

jokes.text: Out of your mouth, Ralph. Volunteers.
jokes.setup: Ralph's confusion about 'oral'
jokes.punchline: Teacher's matter-of-fact, slightly exasperated explanation
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response, shows teacher's weary competence

dialogue.speaker: Ralph Wiggum
dialogue.line: What's 'oral'?

dialogue.speaker: Miss Hoover
dialogue.line: It means spoken, Ralph. As opposed to written.

dialogue.speaker: Ralph Wiggum
dialogue.line: Ohhh.

jokes.text: Thank you, Lisa. - No, Miss Hoover. I'm Ralph.
jokes.setup: Teacher appears to call on Lisa
jokes.punchline: Ralph correcting that he's Ralph, not Lisa
jokes.explanation: Classic Ralph confusion, good misdirection setup

dialogue.speaker: Miss Hoover
dialogue.line: Lisa?

dialogue.speaker: Ralph Wiggum
dialogue.line: I'm Ralph.

jokes.text: Miss Hoover, which one is 'one'?
jokes.setup: Teacher says she only needs one volunteer
jokes.punchline: Ralph now confused about the concept of 'one'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Ralph's confusion gets progressively more basic

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Krabappel
dialogue.line: I only need one volunteer.

dialogue.speaker: Ralph Wiggum
dialogue.line: Ooh! Ooh! Pick me! Pick me!

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Krabappel
dialogue.line: Ralph, I said I only need ONE.

dialogue.speaker: Ralph Wiggum
dialogue.line: I know, but I'm a volunteer!

jokes.text: These are in honor of his army days. - That explains the flags. - What about the dog food? - My theory is Skinner likes dog food.
jokes.setup: Bart making refreshments for Skinner's party
jokes.punchline: Bart's bizarre logic for including dog food in party refreshments
jokes.explanation: Great Bart logic - connecting military service to dog food through child reasoning

dialogue.speaker: Bart Simpson
dialogue.line: I put dog food in the punch bowl because it's got all the nutrients dogs need, and dogs are pretty smart, so it's basically brain food for the party.

jokes.text: Ooh! A fresh batch of America balls!
jokes.setup: Bart has made round patriotic snacks
jokes.punchline: Unfortunate naming of the patriotic treats
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart oblivious double entendre, good child humor

dialogue.speaker: Bart Simpson
dialogue.line: I made these patriotic treats! I'm calling them 'Freedom Fries'... wait, that's already taken. How about 'Liberty Loaves'? No... 'Star-Spangled Snacks'? Hmm, 'Patriot Pucks'? I got it! 'Mini America Meatballs'!

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Those look like turds, boy.

jokes.text: No! Cutting out your ugly nose gives me a hand cramp!
jokes.setup: Seymour mentions silhouette night
jokes.punchline: Mother's brutal honesty about his appearance during supposed bonding activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Agnes - combines maternal activity with casual cruelty, great character integration

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: Mother, I thought silhouette night would be a nice bonding activity for us.

dialogue.speaker: Agnes Skinner
dialogue.line: Seymour, even in silhouette, you look like a nervous little man.

jokes.text: Ralph. - Principal Skinner is an old man who lives at the school.
jokes.setup: Ralph supposed to give biographical report on Skinner
jokes.punchline: Child's literal, basic observation instead of proper biography
jokes.explanation: Pure Ralph - completely missing the point with child logic

dialogue.speaker: Ralph Wiggum
dialogue.line: My dad is a principal.

jokes.text: Teacher made me go to Principal Skinner's office when I was dirty.
jokes.setup: Lisa reading heroic war biography
jokes.punchline: Ralph's completely unrelated personal anecdote
jokes.explanation: Good Ralph non-sequitur, contrasts nicely with serious biography

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: This is a fascinating account of the Battle of Gettysburg.

dialogue.speaker: Ralph Wiggum
dialogue.line: My dad says that war is a lot like love. He learned that in the army.

jokes.text: When I grow up, I wanna be a principal or a caterpillar.
jokes.setup: Ralph considering future career options
jokes.punchline: Equating professional position with insect lifecycle stage
jokes.explanation: Classic Ralph absurdism - completely illogical career aspirations delivered with perfect innocent confidence

dialogue.speaker: Ralph Wiggum
dialogue.line: When I grow up, I'm going to be a caterpillar.

dialogue.speaker: Bart Simpson
dialogue.line: Ralph, caterpillars don't have jobs.

dialogue.speaker: Ralph Wiggum
dialogue.line: They will when I'm done with them!

jokes.text: Nevertheless, I have a surprise for you.
jokes.setup: Skinner acknowledging it's Friday but they normally serve cake Thursdays
jokes.punchline: Formal, bureaucratic language for simple gesture
jokes.explanation: Good Skinner characterization - overly formal for simple things

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: Yes, well, I'm aware it's Friday. However, our standard cake service is Thursday. Therefore, this represents an unauthorized deviation from protocol. I'll need to file a Form 27-B in triplicate before we can proceed with any celebratory confectionery consumption.

jokes.text: An enjoyment buzzer.
jokes.setup: Someone gets buzzed by joy buzzer
jokes.punchline: Skinner's formal, educational name for a simple prank toy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner - making simple pranks sound like educational tools

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: Ah yes, the joy buzzer — or as we call it in the education community, a 'tactile-based, hand-proximity-activated startle mechanism.'

jokes.text: Keep looking shocked and move slowly towards the cake.
jokes.setup: Dramatic revelation that Skinner is an impostor
jokes.punchline: Homer's internal monologue focuses on cake instead of shocking news
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer moment - completely missing emotional weight of situation, perfect character priorities

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: An impostor? But that's impossible! This changes everything! Wait... do they have cake at the town hall?

jokes.text: That's not a funny story.
jokes.setup: Real Skinner calls his POW/slave labor story 'kind of funny'
jokes.punchline: Agnes's blunt assessment of obviously tragic story
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy - stating the obvious about inappropriate humor

dialogue.speaker: Agnes Skinner
dialogue.line: Kind of funny? Seymour, that's the saddest thing I've ever heard. You were a prisoner of war!

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: Well, Mother, I was trying to put a positive spin on it.

jokes.text: Well, I guess you had to be there.
jokes.setup: Agnes points out POW story isn't funny
jokes.punchline: Standard comedy response applied to horrific personal experience
jokes.explanation: Dark humor - casual response to trauma, good subversion of comedy cliche

dialogue.speaker: Agnes Skinner
dialogue.line: That's not funny, Seymour.

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: Well, maybe not to you, Mother. But to the men in the P.O.W. camp, my humorous anecdotes were a welcome respite from the daily beatings and torture.

jokes.text: Well, obviously it isn't. My real name is Armin Tamzarian.
jokes.setup: Asked if Seymour is his real name
jokes.punchline: Reveals completely unexpected ethnic name
jokes.explanation: Major plot twist with inherently funny name choice

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: No, my real name is Armin Tamzarian.

jokes.text: Of course, if I'd known there was a war going on, I probably would've apologized.
jokes.setup: Tamzarian had choice between jail, army, or apologizing
jokes.punchline: Retroactive regret about choosing army over simple apology
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about poor decision-making, relatable regret

dialogue.speaker: Armin Tamzarian
dialogue.line: You know, I had three choices: jail, the army, or apologize to the town.

dialogue.speaker: Armin Tamzarian
dialogue.line: In retrospect, I should have just apologized.

jokes.text: My dreams all involve combing my hair.
jokes.setup: Sergeant asking about his dreams and aspirations
jokes.punchline: Completely shallow, appearance-focused 'dream'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - shows Tamzarian's superficial priorities

dialogue.speaker: Sergeant
dialogue.line: So, Armin, what are your dreams and aspirations?

dialogue.speaker: Armin Tamzarian
dialogue.line: Well, I'd like to have really nice abs, and maybe get a spray tan. Oh, and I want to be really good at texting.

jokes.text: Well, there's nothing corny about fresh-faced youngsters skipping to school... scraping knees and spelling bees... and pies cooling softly on the windowsill.
jokes.setup: Tamzarian calls teaching dreams 'corny'
jokes.punchline: Overly idealized, Norman Rockwell vision of education
jokes.explanation: Good satire of overly sentimental educational ideals

dialogue.speaker: Armen Tamzarian
dialogue.line: Corny? Teaching dreams are corny?

dialogue.speaker: Armen Tamzarian
dialogue.line: I have an overly idealized, Norman Rockwell vision of education!

jokes.text: Well, sir, if that's corny, then corn me up.
jokes.setup: Discussion about whether educational dreams are 'corny'
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic embrace of corniness with made-up phrase
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay moment showing character transformation

dialogue.speaker: Armin Tamzarian
dialogue.line: Well, I think education dreams are wonderful, not corny at all!

dialogue.speaker: Armin Tamzarian
dialogue.line: In fact, I'm going to go out there and say it loud and proud: I'm corn-tastic about learning!

jokes.text: Come on! Get to the part where you steal his identity.
jokes.setup: Tamzarian giving emotional backstory
jokes.punchline: Bart cutting through sentiment to get to the juicy plot point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart - cutting through melodrama with kid's directness

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: I was born in the dark, in a dank alley behind a burlesque house. My mother was a seamstress, my father a sailor. I never knew him. I was raised by wolves, then by nuns, then by a traveling circus...

dialogue.speaker: Bart Simpson
dialogue.line: Dude, did you kill anybody?

jokes.text: I'm trying to explain how emotionally fragile I was.
jokes.setup: Bart wants him to skip to identity theft
jokes.punchline: Tamzarian's wounded response about needing to explain his feelings
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - shows Tamzarian's need for validation

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Skip to the identity theft part!

dialogue.speaker: Armin Tamzarian
dialogue.line: No! I need to explain my feelings first. You can't just skip to the identity theft!

jokes.text: Oh, it's one of those stories.
jokes.setup: Tamzarian insists on explaining emotional fragility
jokes.punchline: Bart's weary recognition of overly emotional storytelling
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about narrative types, perfect Bart cynicism

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: You see, Bart, emotional fragility is a delicate matter that requires understanding and compassion...

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, whatever man. Are we done here?

jokes.text: That's pretty much what I was planning to do there, Armin.
jokes.setup: Tamzarian says 'if you must blame someone, blame me'
jokes.punchline: Skinner's matter-of-fact agreement to blame him
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response to obvious self-blame

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: If you must blame someone, blame me.

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: Alright.

jokes.text: I didn't dream anything about an aqua suit... or a lavender shirt, but you did take care of Mom.
jokes.setup: Tamzarian says he lived out Skinner's dream
jokes.punchline: Skinner critiquing the specific fashion choices in his dream fulfillment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - shows real Skinner's different priorities/taste

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: You lived out my dream? Well, how was I dressed?

dialogue.speaker: Tamzarian
dialogue.line: You were wearing a leisure suit.

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: A leisure suit?! In my dream I'd at least have the good taste to wear a nice blazer!

jokes.text: I have no son.
jokes.setup: Both men trying to comfort her
jokes.punchline: Agnes rejecting both sons equally
jokes.explanation: Perfect Agnes cruelty - rejecting both options rather than choosing

dialogue.speaker: Seymour
dialogue.line: Mother, please, let me help you.

dialogue.speaker: Rod
dialogue.line: Yeah, Mama, we're here for you.

dialogue.speaker: Agnes Skinner
dialogue.line: Oh, shut up, both of you! You're both disappointments!

jokes.text: Look, lady, you obviously have at least one son.
jokes.setup: Agnes claims to have no son with two men claiming to be her son
jokes.punchline: Logical observation about the mathematical impossibility
jokes.explanation: Good logic-based humor pointing out obvious contradiction

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: But Mother, if you have no son, then I shouldn't exist!

dialogue.speaker: Agnes Skinner
dialogue.line: That's right.

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: But I do exist!

dialogue.speaker: Agnes Skinner
dialogue.line: Not in my will, you don't.

jokes.text: No! I have one stranger and one fraud.
jokes.setup: Skinner points out she must have at least one son
jokes.punchline: Agnes's creative categorization to avoid having sons
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Agnes logic - reframing the situation to maintain her rejection while being technically accurate

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: Mother, you must have at least one son.

dialogue.speaker: Agnes Skinner
dialogue.line: Well, I had three boys, but I don't count them as sons.

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: Why not?

dialogue.speaker: Agnes Skinner
dialogue.line: One was a girl.

jokes.text: I don't see how me signing Homer's name is any different... from you using Sergeant Skinner's name.
jokes.setup: Bart caught forging report card, Tamzarian says it's wrong
jokes.punchline: Bart drawing parallel between his fraud and Tamzarian's identity theft
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bart logic - using adult's hypocrisy to justify his own bad behavior, perfect character moment

dialogue.speaker: Principal Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: This is fraud, Bart. You forged your father's signature on your report card.

dialogue.speaker: Bart Simpson
dialogue.line: Well, at least I'm not stealing someone else's identity.

dialogue.speaker: Principal Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: That's different!

dialogue.speaker: Bart Simpson
dialogue.line: Is it though?

jokes.text: I guess me punishing you would be somewhat hypocritical.
jokes.setup: Bart points out parallel between forgery and identity theft
jokes.punchline: Tamzarian's understated admission that punishment would be hypocritical
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to Bart's observation, shows character's self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Bart Simpson
dialogue.line: Wait, isn't what you did basically identity theft?

dialogue.speaker: Armin Tamzarian
dialogue.line: Well... yes. I suppose it is.

dialogue.speaker: Armin Tamzarian
dialogue.line: Which would make punishing you for forgery rather... hypocritical of me.

jokes.text: Hey, hey, hey. Easy there, you big impostor.
jokes.setup: Tamzarian still gives him essay assignment despite hypocrisy
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual use of 'impostor' as nickname/insult
jokes.explanation: Good Bart moment - turning serious revelation into casual insult

dialogue.speaker: Bart Simpson
dialogue.line: Yeah, well at least I'm not an impostor, Impostor.

jokes.text: Just put an 'X,' then call yourself whatever the hell you want.
jokes.setup: Bart asks about signing his original name
jokes.punchline: Tamzarian's defeated, nihilistic response about identity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tamzarian's complete identity crisis

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Wait, so you're saying you were never actually Seymour Skinner?

dialogue.speaker: Armin Tamzarian
dialogue.line: That's right. I'm Armin Tamzarian. I've always been Armin Tamzarian.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: So... should I call you Mr. Tamzarian now?

dialogue.speaker: Armin Tamzarian
dialogue.line: It doesn't matter what you call me, boy. I've spent so many years being someone else that I don't even know who I am anymore. What's in a name anyway? Just letters. Meaningless letters that society uses to cage us into false identities. Call me Tamzarian, call me Skinner, call me nothing at all. It's all the same void.

jokes.text: Who's Ned Flanders?
jokes.setup: Homer mentions how he'd feel if Ned Flanders was impostor
jokes.punchline: Skinner doesn't know Homer's neighbor despite living in town
jokes.explanation: Good detail showing Skinner's disconnect from community

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'd be so angry if Ned Flanders turned out to be an impostor!

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: Who's Ned Flanders?

jokes.text: This sentence I'm speaking... right now. / Period.
jokes.setup: Armin announcing his retirement effective at the end of 'this sentence'
jokes.punchline: The awkward extension of the sentence and literal 'Period'
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor about the arbitrary nature of 'ending a sentence' - the literal 'Period' is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Armin Tamzarian
dialogue.line: I am announcing my retirement effective at the end of this sentence which I am hereby extending with numerous clauses and subordinate phrases as well as tertiary clauses and a seemingly endless series of clauses upon clauses, effectively prolonging the sentence to such a degree that my retirement date has now become indefinite and therefore I shall continue in my current position until such time as I am able to complete this sentence... period.

jokes.text: Armin Tamzarian's reign of terror is over.
jokes.setup: Tamzarian resigning as principal
jokes.punchline: Dramatically overstating the impact of mild-mannered principal
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - treating bureaucratic change like overthrowing dictator

dialogue.speaker: Superintendent Chalmers
dialogue.line: Seymour, you can't resign! Without you, Springfield Elementary will descend into chaos! The students will run wild, the teachers will abandon all discipline, the very foundations of education will crumble!

dialogue.speaker: Principal Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: Superintendent, it's just a school.

dialogue.speaker: Superintendent Chalmers
dialogue.line: Just a school?! Just a SCHOOL?! Seymour, you're the backbone of this institution! Without your firm but fair leadership, we'll see anarchy! Roving gangs of second-graders! Teachers setting fires in the lounge! The very concept of learning will become meaningless!

jokes.text: Oh, 10 Canadian dollars. That's a souvenir from your trip to Upstate New York.
jokes.setup: Going through Skinner's personal belongings
jokes.punchline: Hilariously wrong geography - Canadian money from New York trip
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail - completely wrong geography delivered matter-of-factly

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: Canadian money from my New York trip? But I've never been to Canada!

dialogue.speaker: Superintendent Chalmers
dialogue.line: Skinner, you got Canadian money in New York. That's not how geography works.

jokes.text: I'll wind her every day.
jokes.setup: Getting a pocket watch with mother's picture
jokes.punchline: The ambiguous 'her' - the watch or his mother
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay on 'winding' someone up vs. winding a watch - fits Skinner's formal speech.

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Ah, a pocket watch with mother's picture inside. She's beautiful.

dialogue.speaker: Other Character
dialogue.line: You mean the watch or your mother?

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Yes.

jokes.text: They're full of tiny bones, so I want you to be careful.
jokes.setup: Agnes giving Tamzarian food for his trip
jokes.punchline: Motherly concern despite having rejected him as son
jokes.explanation: Good Agnes moment - maternal instincts despite emotional rejection

dialogue.speaker: Agnes Skinner
dialogue.line: Here, take these sandwiches for your trip.

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Tamzarian
dialogue.line: Thank you, Mother.

dialogue.speaker: Agnes Skinner
dialogue.line: And wear a sweater — it's cold out there.

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Tamzarian
dialogue.line: I thought you didn't want me as your son.

dialogue.speaker: Agnes Skinner
dialogue.line: I don't. But I won't have people saying Agnes Skinner raised an ungrateful boy who catches pneumonia.

jokes.text: No, please. I don't care what you've done. You're still a decent, honorable man... But instead, I'm leaving town forever.
jokes.setup: Edna's romantic plea that she doesn't care about his past
jokes.punchline: Tamzarian completely ignoring her offer and sticking to leaving
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - romantic moment undercut by stubborn determination

dialogue.speaker: Edna Krabappel
dialogue.line: I don't care about your past, Armin. I love you for who you are now.

dialogue.speaker: Armin Tamzarian
dialogue.line: That's very kind of you, but I've made up my mind. I'm leaving Springfield.

dialogue.speaker: Edna Krabappel
dialogue.line: But Armin, we could have a life together!

dialogue.speaker: Armin Tamzarian
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, Edna. My decision is final.

jokes.text: Up yours, children.
jokes.setup: Children calling out to their former principal
jokes.punchline: Tamzarian's hostile response to innocent children
jokes.explanation: Shocking character reversal - mild-mannered principal becoming hostile to children

dialogue.speaker: Children
dialogue.line: Principal Tamzarian! Principal Tamzarian!

dialogue.speaker: Armin Tamzarian
dialogue.line: Get away from me, you little monsters! I'm not your principal anymore!

jokes.text: Not if you called 'em stench blossoms.
jokes.setup: Lisa quotes 'rose by any other name' saying
jokes.punchline: Bart's creative alternative name proving names do matter
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay and philosophical rebuttal from Bart

dialogue.speaker: Lisa Simpson
dialogue.line: A rose by any other name would smell as sweet.

dialogue.speaker: Bart Simpson
dialogue.line: Yeah, but call it a stinkweed and see how many people stop to smell it.

jokes.text: Or crap weeds.
jokes.setup: Bart suggests 'stench blossoms' as alternative name
jokes.punchline: Homer's even worse flower name
jokes.explanation: Good escalation building on Bart's joke

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: How about 'crotch crickets'?

jokes.text: Not if they were called scum drops.
jokes.setup: Lisa mentions candy as preferable to bad-named flowers
jokes.punchline: Marge applying same logic to candy names
jokes.explanation: Continues the naming theme but less inspired than earlier entries

dialogue.speaker: Marge Simpson
dialogue.line: Well, if we're going to name candy, we should name it something appealing. Like 'Delicious Chocolate Nuggets' instead of 'Cocoa Nuggets.'

dialogue.speaker: Marge Simpson
dialogue.line: Or 'Sweet Fruity Spheres' instead of whatever those terrible names are.

jokes.text: Hey, America- America.
jokes.setup: Asked to lead Pledge of Allegiance
jokes.punchline: Bart's minimal, disrespectful attempt at pledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart disrespect - minimal effort while technically complying

dialogue.speaker: Bart Simpson
dialogue.line: I pledge allegiance to the flag... of the United States of America.

jokes.text: You haven't dealt with women for a long time, have you, Sergeant?
jokes.setup: Sergeant asks if Edna is asking him out
jokes.punchline: Edna's observation about his poor social skills
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing social awkwardness of military man

dialogue.speaker: Sergeant Skinner
dialogue.line: Are you asking me out?

dialogue.speaker: Edna Krabappel
dialogue.line: God, no. I'm just saying you have the social skills of a hermit crab.

jokes.text: That child is the most disobedient, smart-alecky, middle-aged man in creation!
jokes.setup: Neighbor asking about her new son
jokes.punchline: Agnes describing adult man as disobedient child
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Agnes line - treating grown man like misbehaving child, perfect character voice

dialogue.speaker: Neighbor
dialogue.line: Agnes, I heard you had a new son!

dialogue.speaker: Agnes Skinner
dialogue.line: Yes, I adopted a 47-year-old man. He's very disobedient.

dialogue.speaker: Neighbor
dialogue.line: A 47-year-old man? How is he disobedient?

dialogue.speaker: Agnes Skinner
dialogue.line: He won't eat his vegetables, he stays up past his bedtime, and he keeps asking for an allowance!

jokes.text: Twelve, 11, 10. The man's a weenie.
jokes.setup: Counting items in express lane
jokes.punchline: Judging Skinner's character based on grocery lane choice
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - petty judgment revealing personality

dialogue.speaker: Edna Krabappel
dialogue.line: That's ten items. This is an express lane.

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: I know, but—

dialogue.speaker: Edna Krabappel
dialogue.line: You're a man who takes what he wants, regardless of the rules. I find that very attractive.

jokes.text: Twelve, 11, 10. The man's a weenie.
jokes.setup: Counting items while discussing the principal
jokes.punchline: The matter-of-fact delivery of 'weenie' while counting
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery mixing mundane counting with character judgment.

dialogue.speaker: Cashier
dialogue.line: That'll be $8.95. By the way, how's Principal Skinner doing?

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: Oh, he's fine. Why do you ask?

dialogue.speaker: Cashier
dialogue.line: One weenie, two weenies, three weenies...

jokes.text: But he was our weenie.
jokes.setup: Comparison between Tamzarian and Skinner as weenies
jokes.punchline: Preference for familiar weenie over unknown weenie
jokes.explanation: Perfect sentiment about preferring familiar flaws to unknown ones

dialogue.speaker: Edna Krabappel
dialogue.line: I prefer a familiar weenie over an unknown weenie.

jokes.text: 'Cause Jasper didn't wanna come by himself.
jokes.setup: Chain of explanations for why everyone is in the car
jokes.punchline: Final explanation reveals Jasper's social needs
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to explanation chain, reveals character dependencies

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: We're driving Jasper around because he's lonely.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: We're doing it because Homer promised Jasper we would.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: We're doing it because Mom said we had to.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: We're doing it because Dad felt guilty.

dialogue.speaker: Grampa
dialogue.line: We're doing it because Jasper asked real nice-like, and nobody ever asks old Jasper for nothin'.

jokes.text: Hello, beautiful. [Snoring] / Hello, beautiful. [Snoring Resumes]
jokes.setup: Grampa hitting on someone in his sleep
jokes.punchline: Continuing the pickup line while unconscious
jokes.explanation: Classic Grampa humor - his subconscious romantic inclinations continuing even while asleep.

dialogue.speaker: Grampa Simpson
dialogue.line: Are you a magician? Because whenever I look at you, everyone else disappears... Mmm, disappears...

jokes.text: This is Armin's apartment... Armin's liquor, Armin's copy of Swank, Armin's frozen peas.
jokes.setup: Declaring his independence and new identity
jokes.punchline: Claiming ownership of increasingly mundane items including adult magazine
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from meaningful to mundane possessions

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: I'm not Principal Skinner anymore. I'm Armin Tamzarian, and I'm taking back my life!

dialogue.speaker: Armin Tamzarian
dialogue.line: This is my house now.

dialogue.speaker: Armin Tamzarian
dialogue.line: This is my car.

dialogue.speaker: Armin Tamzarian
dialogue.line: And this... this is my adult magazine.

jokes.text: Can I see your copy of Swank, Armin?
jokes.setup: Tamzarian mentions his adult magazine
jokes.punchline: Bart's innocent interest in inappropriate material
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart - inappropriate curiosity delivered innocently

dialogue.speaker: Tamzarian
dialogue.line: I've got some adult magazines in my office.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Cool! Can I read about the stock market and the political situation in the Middle East?

jokes.text: Yes, you can.
jokes.setup: Bart asks to see adult magazine
jokes.punchline: Tamzarian's casual agreement to show child inappropriate material
jokes.explanation: Shows Tamzarian's lack of proper authority/judgment

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Can I see that magazine?

dialogue.speaker: Armin Tamzarian
dialogue.line: Sure, why not.

jokes.text: But I'm a hero!
jokes.setup: Being put on train and told not to come back
jokes.punchline: Desperate appeal to war hero status
jokes.explanation: Good desperation moment - credentials meaningless in face of social preference

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: But surely you won't send away a decorated war hero?

dialogue.speaker: Conductor
dialogue.line: You were in the Army Closet Corps.

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: It still counts!

jokes.text: It's nice you're alive, but you're just not what I'm looking for in a son.
jokes.setup: Real son being shipped away
jokes.punchline: Agnes treating son selection like job interview
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant Agnes moment - casual cruelty delivered like HR rejection, perfect dark comedy

dialogue.speaker: Agnes Skinner
dialogue.line: Well, this one has a nice firm handshake. I'll take him!

jokes.text: I've never been happier or prouder to be Seymour Skinner... but these last few days as that hotheaded rogue Armin Tamzarian... have taught me a thing or two.
jokes.setup: Back to being Skinner, reflecting on experience
jokes.punchline: Describing mild-mannered self as 'hotheaded rogue'
jokes.explanation: Good self-perception humor - viewing mundane behavior as rebellious

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: Well, I may have had a brief identity crisis, but I've returned to being the mild-mannered Principal Seymour Skinner.

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: Though I must say, that whole experience as the hotheaded rogue Armin Tamzarian was quite exhilarating.

jokes.text: Oh, no, we won't!
jokes.setup: Skinner saying they'll see a new Seymour Skinner
jokes.punchline: Agnes immediately shutting down any change
jokes.explanation: Perfect Agnes control - immediately crushing any hint of independence

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: You'll see a new Seymour Skinner.

dialogue.speaker: Agnes Skinner
dialogue.line: No, I won't.

jokes.text: Oh, no, we won't! / Yes, Mother.
jokes.setup: Skinner promising to be less uptight
jokes.punchline: Agnes immediately shutting down any change, Skinner's instant compliance
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - shows that despite everything, nothing has really changed. Perfect economy and character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: Mother, I promise I'll try to be less uptight and more spontaneous.

dialogue.speaker: Agnes Skinner
dialogue.line: Absolutely not.

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: Yes, Mother.

tmdb_overview: Unmasked as an impostor, Principal Skinner flees to Capital City to reclaim his former life as a shiftless good-for-nothing. The people of Springfield gain a newfound appreciation for him and form a plan to bring him back.
insight: Principal Seymour's identity crisis generates 63 jokes in 22 minutes — Season 9's densest episode yet.
